channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Madonna’s ‘I Feel So Free’ Officially Released Following Surprise Coachella Appearance With Sabrina Carpenter
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to the latest music update. We've got exciting news for all you Madonna fans out there. The Queen of Pop has just dropped a brand new song, and we're diving into all the details."
[Short music transition]
Host: "The song, titled 'I Feel So Free', debuted on iHeartRadio's Pride station on Friday, April 17, and was later released on streaming services at midnight. But that's not all - Madonna also surprised fans with a live appearance during Sabrina Carpenter's Coachella headlining set."
[Short music clip from "I Feel So Free"]
Host: "So, what can we expect from 'I Feel So Free'? It's a pulsating dance track that takes inspiration from the 1989 house classic 'French Kiss' by Lil Louis. The song also echoes the sound of Madonna's 2005 electro-pop era, specifically her hit 'Future Lovers'."
[Short music transition]
Host: "But here's the thing - 'I Feel So Free' is just a teaser for something bigger. It's part of Madonna's upcoming album, Confessions II, which is set to drop on July 3. This album is the sequel to her 2005 hit Confessions on a Dance Floor, and it marks her first release since re-signing with Warner Records in 2025."
[Short music clip from "I Feel So Free"]
Host: "Confessions II is shaping up to be a major release, with 16 tracks and a reunion with producer Stuart Price, who worked on the original Confessions album. We can't wait to hear more from Madonna's latest project. Stay tuned for updates, and get ready to dance the summer away with the Queen of Pop."
[Outro music plays]
